text: HR RAG snippets for disambiguating HR queries to correct columns; focus on locations vs departments and grade normalization; generated 2025-12-10.

Crosswalk from ODBC to employees table: DEPARTMENT_NAME -> employees.department (functional area); LOCATION_NAME -> employees.branch_name (physical site such as UBL Head Office); REGION/DISTRICT/BRANCH -> employees.branch_region or branch_city; BRANCH -> employees.branch_name when populated; GRADE -> employees.grade_level; EMPLOYMENT_STATUS -> employees.status; DATE_OF_JOIN -> employees.hire_date; ASSIGNMENT_START_DATE -> employees.hire_date when populated; GROSS_SALARY -> employees.salary; MANAGER_EMP_NO -> employees.manager_id; JOB_NAME or POSITION_NAME -> employees.job_role; DEPT_GROUP or SUB_GROUP -> employees.unit_name; CONTRACT_TYPE -> employees.employment_type; ACTION_REASON/ACTION_CODE -> employees.termination_reason when termination is present; EMAIL_ADDRESS -> employees.email_address; CONTACT_NUMBER -> employees.phone; NATIONALITY -> employees.nationality; RELIGION/MARITAL_STATUS -> same fields; CNIC_NATIONAL_ID -> store in metadata if needed.
Canonical grade_level values: SC-1, SVP-I, SVP-II, CON, AVP-I, AVP-II, OG-1, OG-2, OG-3, OG-4, RVP, VP, SEVP-I, SEVP-II.

Grade aliases map to canonical: avp1/avp 1/avp-1/avp-i -> AVP-I; avp2/avp 2/avp-2/avp-ii -> AVP-II; svp1/svp 1/svp-1/svp-i -> SVP-I; svp2/svp 2/svp-2/svp-ii -> SVP-II; og1 -> OG-1; og2 -> OG-2; og3 -> OG-3; og4 -> OG-4; sc1/sc-1 -> SC-1; rsvps -> SVP-I.

Location fields in employees table: branch_country (country), branch_city (city), branch_name (physical site or branch e.g., UBL Head Office), branch_region (region/zone), branch_code (short code). Use these for location queries; they are not departments.

Departmental fields in employees table: department (functional department), unit_name (sub-unit or team), job_role (position title), job_level_category (job ladder or band). Do not confuse with physical locations.

Organizational structure columns from ODBC: DEPARTMENT_NAME, DEPARTMENT_TYPE, DEPT_GROUP, SUB_GROUP, DIVISION, CLUSTERS, REGION, DISTRICT, BRANCH, BRANCH_LEVEL, BRANCH_FLAGSHIP, LOCATION_NAME, POSITION_CODE, POSITION_NAME, JOB_NAME, CADRE. LOCATION_NAME and BRANCH describe physical place; DEPARTMENT_NAME/TYPE/DEPT_GROUP/SUB_GROUP/DIVISION describe organizational ownership.

Contact and identity columns: EMPLOYEE_NUMBER (business key), ASSIGNMENT_NUMBER, EMPLOYEE_FULL_NAME, TITLE, FATHER_NAME, NATIONALITY, CNIC_NATIONAL_ID, PHONE_TYPE, CONTACT_NUMBER, EMAIL_TYPE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS_TYPE, HOME_ADDRESS, BLOOD_GROUP, RELIGION, MARITAL_STATUS, GENDER, DATE_OF_BIRTH.

Assignment and lifecycle dates: DATE_OF_JOIN, ASSIGNMENT_START_DATE, ASSIGNMENT_END_DATE, DATE_PROBATION_END, PROBATION_DURATION, CONFIRMED_DATE, LAST_WORKING_DATE, ACTUAL_TERMINATION_DATE, NOTIFIED_TERMINATION_DATE, PROJECTED_TERMINATION_DATE, CONTRACT_END_DATE. These relate to employment timeline, not to demographics or location.

Status fields: EMPLOYMENT_STATUS (Active, Resigned, etc.), PERSON_TYPE, ACTION_CODE, ACTION_REASON, NOTICE_PERIOD, LEAVE_FLAG, CANCEL_WORKRELSHIP_FLAG. Use EMPLOYMENT_STATUS for current state and ACTION_REASON for why a change occurred.

Compensation/benefit columns: GROSS_SALARY (numeric), FESTIVAL_FLAG, GRATUITY, SPI_FLAG, PF_CONVERTED, PF_CON_DATE, PF, PF_AMEEN, GPF, PENSION, COMP_ABS, POST_RET_MED, BF, REL_ALL, UNION_MEM, EXTG_FUEL_ENTIT_OR_BMC, EXPENSE_POP. GROSS_SALARY maps to employees.salary for pay queries.
Manager linkage columns: MANAGER_EMP_NAME, MANAGER_EMP_NO indicate reporting manager; use MANAGER_EMP_NO to join to employees.manager_id when normalizing data.

total_experience table columns: EMPLOYEE_NUMBER, TITLE, FULL_NAME, Grade, Hiring_date, USER_STATUS, Total_Experience, Previous_Employer, Postion (position). Use Total_Experience for tenure outside the bank and combine with DATE_OF_JOIN for overall experience.

question: How many employees are in UBL Head Office?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS employee_count FROM employees WHERE branch_name = 'UBL Head Office';
text: For location names like UBL Head Office, filter on branch_name (or ODBC.LOCATION_NAME), not department.

question: List female employees in Karachi branch offices
sql: SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, branch_name FROM employees WHERE branch_city = 'Karachi' AND gender = 'F';
text: City-based queries should use branch_city. branch_name holds the site (e.g., UBL Head Office, Clifton Branch).

question: Show average salary for avp 1 in Risk department
sql: SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees WHERE department = 'Risk' AND grade_level = 'AVP-I' GROUP BY department;
text: Normalize avp 1/avp-i to canonical grade_level AVP-I before filtering.

question: Count employees in Operations unit named Trade Services
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE department = 'Operations' AND unit_name = 'Trade Services';
text: department is the broad area; unit_name is the sub-unit. Keep them separate from location fields.

question: Find employees in the North region and flagship branches
sql: SELECT EMPLOYEE_NUMBER, EMPLOYEE_FULL_NAME, LOCATION_NAME, REGION, BRANCH_FLAGSHIP FROM odbc WHERE REGION = 'North' AND BRANCH_FLAGSHIP = 'Y';
text: In ODBC, REGION and BRANCH_FLAGSHIP describe territory and branch type. LOCATION_NAME is the branch or office.

question: List employees terminated for redundancy
sql: SELECT EMPLOYEE_NUMBER, EMPLOYEE_FULL_NAME, ACTION_REASON, ACTUAL_TERMINATION_DATE FROM odbc WHERE ACTION_REASON ILIKE '%redundancy%';
text: Termination reasons live in ACTION_REASON, while ACTUAL_TERMINATION_DATE is the end date. Combine with EMPLOYMENT_STATUS if needed.

question: Show employees with more than 10 years total experience
sql: SELECT EMPLOYEE_NUMBER, FULL_NAME, Total_Experience FROM total_experience WHERE CAST(Total_Experience AS FLOAT) > 10;
text: Use total_experience.Total_Experience for pre-join experience; join to employees on EMPLOYEE_NUMBER -> employee_id if merged.
